title: Tell time on the number line
description: Match time of a number line to time on an analog or digital clock. Graph a given time on a number line.
topic_path: Module 2: Place value and problem solving with units of measure
Topic A: Time measurement and problem solving
Tell time on the number line

assessment_items.question_content:
Look at the following number line.

[[☃ image 1]]

**What time is shown on the number line?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $ :$ [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
hints: The $\goldD{A}$ is located after the hour of $11$ o'clock but before $12$ o'clock.  So the hour is $11$.

Each tick mark on the number line represents one minute.

The $\goldD{A}$ is located $1$ tick mark after $50$ minutes. So the minutes are $51$.
The time is $11\colon{51}$.

options.alt: A number line labeled 11:00 to 12:00 with tick marks every 1 unit. Major tick marks are labeled at 11:15, 11:30, and 11:45. The sixth tick mark after 11:45 is labeled A.

assessment_items.question_content:
The number line below represents one hour on a clock. 


[[☃ image 1]]

**Where is $7{:}31$ graphed on the number line?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Each tick mark on the number line represents one minute.

$7{:}31$ is $31$ minutes after $7{:}00$.  

So, we move $31$ tick marks past $7{:}00$. 



[[☃ image 1]]

$7{:}31$ is graphed at $\redD{C}$ on the number line.

options.alt: A number line labeled 0 to 60 with tick marks every 1 unit. The 0 tick mark is also labeled 7:00 and the 60 tick mark is also labeled 8:00. The third tick mark after 10 is labeled A. The seventh tick mark after 10 is labeled B. The first tick mark after 30 is labeled C.

choices.content: $\goldD{A}$
$\blueD{B}$
$\redD{C}$
